draft.introduction: Surah Maryam (Mary) narrates the births of John the Baptist and Jesus, honoring Mary as chosen above all women. It is the Quran’s most extended engagement with Christian tradition and its most sympathetic portrait of Jesus.

translation.text: And mention in the Book, Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place facing east. She took a veil apart from them. Then We sent to her Our Spirit, and he appeared to her as a well-proportioned man. She said, ‘Indeed, I seek refuge from you in the Most Merciful.’ He said, ‘I am only the messenger of your Lord to give you a pure son.’

translator_notes.note: The “Spirit” (Ruh) sent to Mary is identified in Islamic tradition as the angel Gabriel. The Quran affirms the virgin birth of Jesus while denying his divinity—he is a prophet and “word” from God, but not the Son of God in the Christian trinitarian sense.
